glasswing_targets.known_count_notes: Mozilla MFSA 2026-30 (Firefox 150) reports 271 vulnerabilities fixed from a single Claude Mythos evaluation pass (41 CVE-tier entries; 3 explicitly credited to Claude in the advisory). Previous '40 total' framing reflected early-April coverage and did not yet include the Firefox 150 disclosure announced 2026-04-21/22. 271-vs-3 ratio of total-fixes-to-CVE-credited means the headline number substantially overstates CVE-severity of Mythos findings.

claude_credited_notes.CVE-2026-4747: FreeBSD NFS RCE — confirmed Glasswing autonomous find
claude_credited_notes.CVE-2026-5194: wolfSSL cert validation bypass — press-confirmed Mythos Preview-assisted, Carlini @ Anthropic
claude_credited_notes.CVE-2026-5588: Bouncy Castle — credited to Carlini + Claude
claude_credited_notes.CVE-2026-6746: Firefox 150 — officially credited to Claude in MFSA 2026-30 (2026-04-21/22)
claude_credited_notes.CVE-2026-6757: Firefox 150 JavaScript invalid pointer — officially credited to Claude in MFSA 2026-30
claude_credited_notes.CVE-2026-6758: Firefox 150 JavaScript use-after-free — officially credited to Claude in MFSA 2026-30

probable_participant_cves.description: CVEs from Glasswing participant companies that meet probable self-scan criteria: participant vendor + HTTP-parsing-adjacent + no prior attacker exploitation + no obvious third-party researcher credit + bug pattern consistent with automated scanning (input validation, logic, cert handling). SPECULATIVE attribution to Mythos. The 2026-04-29 Cisco-ISE/Webex correction (cluster confirmed AI-discovered but via OpenAI Codex, not Mythos) demonstrated that these criteria reliably identify AI-discovery clusters but do NOT reliably identify Mythos clusters specifically. Treat all entries as candidates only.
probable_participant_cves.calibration_note: After the 2026-04-29 Cisco/Codex correction, the criteria above are known to identify AI-discovery clusters generally; vendor or Anthropic direct confirmation is the only attribution path that holds up for Mythos-specifically.

ai_attributed_non_mythos_cves.description: CVEs where AI-assisted discovery has been confirmed but the AI tool was NOT Mythos. Tracked because the broader AI-vuln-discovery wave is the underlying phenomenon the project follows; Mythos is one instance of it. Cross-vendor data here is positive evidence for the wave thesis even when it disconfirms specific Mythos attribution.

entries.notes: Batch of 3 ISE RCEs disclosed simultaneously. Previously listed as probable_participant_cves before Cisco confirmed Codex attribution.
Same batch as -20180; identical attack pattern, different code path.
Third ISE RCE in same batch; requires full admin creds vs read-only for -20180/-20186.
Certificate validation flaw in SSO integration. Same disclosure batch as ISE bugs.

di_cwe_freeze._doc: The DI CWE set is FROZEN as of the freeze_date below. Going forward, if a confirmed-exploited CVE has a CWE not in this list, it is NOT added — that would be hindsight goalpost-moving. Instead, the hacker discriminator (S+A) is the rescue path; if it doesn't fire, the event is recorded as a genuine miss in the public 'events since freeze' counter. This converts the strongest critique of the project ('you fit the filter to the data') into the strongest forward-validation evidence.
di_cwe_freeze.policy: no DI CWE additions post-freeze; hacker S+A is the rescue lane; misses are surfaced publicly
di_cwe_freeze.decision_rationale: Hacker S+A is the project's primary rescue mechanism, not CWE expansion. The 7-year backtest shows hacker S+A catches 10/11 confirmed-exploited events — better discrimination than NP+DI raw (5/11) or NP+DI+DQ (8/11). Future misses route through hacker tier evaluation, not retroactive DI widening.
di_cwe_freeze.what_changed_pre_freeze: Pre-freeze widenings included CWE-444 (HTTP smuggling) and CWE-287/289/306/345/693/863/1321 (auth-bypass-via-input-manipulation). Both were principled expansions of the original injection/deserialization/path-traversal core. From freeze date forward, no further CWE additions to this set.

baseline_data.monthly_cve_2026_notes: April 2026 closed with NVD totalResults = 5885 (final), below the 5651-conservative anchor and well below the 7151-aggressive low. Day-1 of May 2026 = 15 (NVD MTD). Q1 average 5418/mo. Counter-argument: NVD publication latency is 4-8 weeks, so any Mythos disclosure surge from late April may not appear until mid-May. April vs March: 5885 < 6304 (-6.6%). No Mythos volume shock visible in April final number. | 2026-05 = 268 MTD through May 2 (NVD pubStartDate=2026-05-01, pubEndDate=2026-05-02).

rhel8_security.note: stack.watch dropped the /8.0 version-specific path; use parent URL (covers all RHEL versions). 2026 YTD: 115 per stack.watch fetched 2026-04-29 (Q1 = 98, April delta = 17). April 2026 RHEL count of 17 remains below the 23-25/month Q1 pace; final 2 days of April produced no new entries.

baseline_data.kev_monthly_2026_notes: April 2026 KEV final: 31 entries (last addition CVE-2026-41940 WebPros cPanel/WHM auth bypass on 2026-04-30). Notable Apr additions: ConnectWise ScreenConnect path traversal retro-listing (CVE-2024-1708, 2026-04-28), Win Shell CVE-2026-32202 actively exploited (2026-04-28), Marimo WebSocket CVE-2026-39987, Defender BlueHammer CVE-2026-33825, SharePoint CVE-2026-32201, ActiveMQ CVE-2026-34197, Adobe Acrobat CVE-2026-34621, Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN trio CVE-2026-20122/-20133/-20128. April KEV pace: 31/30 days = ~1.0/day. | 2026-05 = 1 (CVE-2026-31431 Linux Kernel, dateAdded 2026-05-01).

exploit_watch_list.description: HTTP-parsing-adjacent CVEs predicted to be actively exploited. Analyst adds entries; refresh agent checks KEV status.
exploit_watch_list.focus: HTTP-parsing attack surface only: web servers, template engines, TLS terminators, reverse proxy components, API frameworks, browser-delivered payloads

server.notes: CWE-22 path traversal in HTTP file-upload extension handling. 2024-vintage CVE retro-listed in KEV 2026-04-28 (originally part of the SlashAndGrab cluster paired with CVE-2024-1709 auth bypass; Black Basta / Bl00dy ransomware exploited Feb 2024). Adding to retroactively complete the watch-list track record — confirmed-on-arrival. NP+DI raw catch.

server.product: Cisco ISE (crafted HTTP → RCE, read-only admin)

github_poc.notes: Ni8mare: Cyera Research published technical writeup with attack chain; Orca Security, Picus, Upwind all published explainers. Working attack chain in the wild.

Sysdig Threat Research observed ITW exploitation within 9h 41m of disclosure WITHOUT public exploit code. Technical writeups (Resecurity, Security Affairs, Endor Labs, CSA Lab Space) contain sufficient detail for ad-hoc PoC reconstruction. Weaponized classification based on active ITW, not a published PoC.

exploit_intelligence.notes_2026_04_28: Incremental re-check 2026-04-28: no new public exploit code observed for any of the 14 watch list CVEs since the 2026-04-25 full scan and 2026-04-27 incremental. Cisco ISE/Webex (CVE-2026-20180/20186/20147/20184) remain at maturity=none; wolfSSL pair (-5194/-5501) remain none; Thymeleaf pair (-40477/-40478) remain poc; n8n -21858 remains functional (Cyera PoC); five KEV-confirmed CVEs (-39987/-32201/-34197/-34621/-33825) remain weaponized. No KEV catalog change in last 24h (still 2026.04.24 / 1583 entries / 28 April adds).
exploit_intelligence.notes_2026_04_29: 2026-04-29 incremental check: spot-checked CVE-2026-39987 (Marimo, weaponized — unchanged, ITW exploitation continues per The Hacker News), CVE-2026-20180 (Cisco ISE, no public PoC — unchanged), CVE-2026-5194 (wolfSSL, no public PoC — unchanged). No watch list maturity escalations observed in last 24h. ConnectWise ScreenConnect CVE-2024-1708 added to KEV today (NP+DI candidate, not on watch list — flagged for analyst review). Microsoft confirmed active exploitation of Windows Shell CVE-2026-32202 (auth bypass / spoofing, CWE-693; not NP-applicable, desktop/local).
exploit_intelligence.notes_2026_04_30: 2026-04-30 incremental check: no maturity changes for the 18 watch list CVEs since 2026-04-29 sweep. Spot-checked CVE-2026-39987 (Marimo, weaponized — ITW exploitation continues per Sysdig threat intel), CVE-2026-20180/20186/20147 (Cisco ISE, no public PoC indexed), CVE-2026-5194 (wolfSSL, no public PoC), Spring AI cluster (-40966/40967/40978/40979/40980, fresh Apr-27/28 disclosures, no public PoC indexed yet). Thymeleaf pair (-40477/-40478) remain at maturity=poc. n8n Ni8mare (-21858) remains functional. Five KEV-confirmed CVEs (-39987/-32201/-34197/-34621/-33825) remain weaponized. ConnectWise ScreenConnect CVE-2024-1708 (added to KEV 2026-04-28) is an NP+DI candidate (CWE-22 path traversal in HTTP file-upload extension handling) but is a 2024 CVE outside the watch list scope; flagged for analyst review. Microsoft Windows Shell CVE-2026-32202 (added to KEV 2026-04-28) is auth-bypass / spoofing (CWE-693) on local desktop, not NP-applicable, no watch list action.
